alerts.title: Trump vows immediate Iran strike; threatens seizure of oil and gas sectors
alerts.summary: US President Trump publicly stated intent to strike Iran 'very hard tonight' and threatened to seize Iranian oil and gas sectors, significantly escalating rhetoric beyond previous diplomatic posture (incidents feed: NPR). Statement directly contradicts ongoing US-Iran framework MoU signing preparations and signals potential reversal of ceasefire commitment.

alerts.title: Israel halts major Iran strike one hour before scheduled takeoff
alerts.summary: Israeli Defense Force Chief confirmed Israel aborted a significant planned airstrike on Iran approximately one hour before execution, reportedly in response to Trump administration pressure to preserve US-Iran peace deal (Ynetnews, incidents feed). Demonstrates acute US-Israel coordination fracture over de-escalation strategy amid ongoing Lebanon strikes.

alerts.title: Iran fires warning shots at vessel in Strait of Hormuz; Qatar covert funding arrangement exposed
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator reported Iran discharged warning shots at a commercial vessel transiting Strait of Hormuz on June 15 (44.8K views). Simultaneously, diplomatic sources confirmed Washington secretly authorized Qatar to transfer funds to Tehran in exchange for freedom of navigation and attack immunity, deliberately structured to reduce global energy prices (~37K views, high-confidence source).

alerts.title: Iran maintains 60% enrichment; breakout window unchanged at 1-2 weeks during framework talks
alerts.summary: Iran continues uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with persistent 1-2 week breakout capability despite active US-Iran framework agreement negotiations (global_metrics). IAEA board resolution on inspection access and uranium stock reporting remains unresolved, indicating nuclear verification dispute has not been addressed in diplomatic breakthrough.

alerts.title: US sentiment on Iran deal shifts positive amid Trump's contradictory messaging
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 53% positive, 38% negative coverage on Iran-deal topics, with 'Trump' (100 mentions), 'Iran' (54), and 'deal' (42) dominating discourse (sentiment feed). However, Trump's new strike threat represents sudden reversal from deal-positive positioning, signaling potential strategic pivot that may destabilize nascent agreement.

analysis_note: US-Iran framework agreement faces imminent collapse risk as Trump escalates military rhetoric and threatens oil sector seizure, directly contradicting ceasefire commitments; Israeli strike resumption on Lebanon and Iranian warning shots in Strait of Hormuz indicate regional de-escalation is fragile.
